intro: Cyathea estelae, also known as the Estela tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Cyathea estelae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyathea estelae is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and also used in the form of tinctures and salves, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be extracted and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
